title: rhinanthus pallidus
intro: Rhinanthus pallidus, also known as the pale foxglove or pale monkey flower, is a flowering plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, such as grasses, and can be found in open habitats and grasslands. The medicinal properties of Rhinanthus pallidus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and support immune function, with herbal preparations typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for consumption.
